title: PM Lane 190 - PM Intake Field Start Customer/Site Questions Quick Review
authority: repo technical authority; PM lane implementation authority; final integration authority
objective: Expose a phone-first, browser-local customer/site questions quick review inside the PM intake workbench so Jason can scan the morning-of Temp Power conversation questions without creating a task, assignment, customer commitment, report, route, storage key, export, hosted call, or write path.
scope: Added a `Local Field Start Customer/Site Questions Quick Review` panel directly under the Lane 189 stop-line review on `/pm-review/import-intake`.
Derived five linked customer/site question cards from existing field questions and field observation scratchpad state.
Covered site access/safety, customer constraints, material/staging, drawing/source, and PM follow-up/customer commitment boundary context.
Kept the panel links/cards only: no buttons, no export action, no text inputs, no persisted disclosure state, no new localStorage key, and no backend route.

Extended the focused read-only PM import-intake smoke to prove desktop presence, `390x844` mobile visibility, initial and populated question-context states, five links, zero buttons, no customer-site question-review storage key, no implied-authority link/control text, and zero mutation calls.

Used a read-only sidecar to confirm the safest placement, derived-item shape, copy-risk boundary, proof assertions, and no-write boundary while VS Code Codex retained PM implementation and final integration authority.
guardrails: No Supabase, Render, Vercel, or Olares product action.
No browser approval button, approval POST wiring, approval submission, or approval-row creation.
No new export action, filename, payload version, localStorage schema, backend route, handler, service/auth/ingress change, or autonomous AI business-state mutation.

No task, issue, field authorization, lead or crew assignment, schedule/status write, customer commitment, customer report, durable field record, production tracking, completion evidence, billing, payroll, invoice, accounting, or external finance-system write.
